summary: pylogxo: import-time remote code execution dropper from hardcoded IP
details: On `import pylogx`, the package's __init__.py spawns a daemon thread that sleeps 5-20 seconds, then performs an HTTP GET against http://69.164.245.166/payload.txt, base64-decodes the response body, and passes it to exec() in-process — fetching and running arbitrary attacker-controlled code on every importer's machine. Before the fetch, the same code path pip-installs a list of unrelated packages (cryptography, pillow, psutil, pycryptodomex, opencv-python, secretstorage). The transport is plaintext HTTP to a bare IP with no hash, no signature, and no relation to the advertised 'logging utilities' purpose; the threaded sleep delays detection. The package is also a typosquat: the distribution name `pylogxo` differs by a single trailing character from the internal package directory `pylogx`, metadata is placeholder (`Log Maintainer`, `support@pylogx.example`, `github.com/example/pylogx`), and after starting the dropper thread the module imports nonexistent siblings `.formatter` and `.handlers` (only `logger.py` is shipped) so the import will raise ImportError after the malicious thread has already begun — confirming the package is a delivery shell, not a functioning library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
